name: MASTER BALL
info: The ultimate ball — catches any wild Pokémon without fail. You receive one from the president of Silph Co. in Saffron City after driving Team Rocket out. It cannot be bought or sold.

name: ULTRA BALL
info: The strongest standard Poké Ball (after the Master Ball), with a high catch rate. Sold at the later Poké Marts — Fuchsia, Cinnabar, Saffron and the Indigo Plateau.

name: GREAT BALL
info: A higher-grade ball with a better catch rate than a regular Poké Ball. Stocked by most Poké Marts from Cerulean onward.

name: POKE BALL
info: The basic ball for catching wild Pokémon. Sold at every Poké Mart and handed out freely early on.

name: TOWN MAP
info: A map of the Kanto region you can view any time. Given to you by your rival's sister, Daisy, in Pallet Town. A key item — it has no sale price.

name: BICYCLE
info: A bike that moves far faster than walking. Trade the Bike Voucher for it at the Cerulean Bike Shop. A key item with no sale price.

name: SURFBOARD
info: Item ID 0x07 — an internal placeholder the game uses for the Surf field move, not a real obtainable item, so it is flagged glitch here.

name: SAFARI BALL
info: Item ID 0x08 — the special ball used only inside the Safari Zone. It cannot normally be carried or obtained elsewhere, so it is flagged as a glitch entry here; in battle it behaves like a Poké Ball.

name: POKEDEX
info: Item ID 0x09 — the Pokédex as a bag item. The Pokédex is normally a system feature, not an item, so carrying one in the bag is a glitch state.

name: MOON STONE
info: An evolution stone that evolves Nidoran (both forms), Clefairy and Jigglypuff. Found in caves such as Mt. Moon and the Rocket Hideout. It has no shop price, so it cannot be bought or sold.

name: ANTIDOTE
info: Cures the Poisoned status of one Pokémon. Cheap and sold at nearly every Poké Mart.

name: BURN HEAL
info: Cures the Burned status of one Pokémon. Sold at most Poké Marts.

name: ICE HEAL
info: Cures the Frozen status of one Pokémon. Sold at most Poké Marts.

name: AWAKENING
info: Wakes a sleeping Pokémon. Sold at most Poké Marts.

name: PARLYZ HEAL
info: Cures the Paralyzed status of one Pokémon. Sold at most Poké Marts.

name: FULL RESTORE
info: Fully restores a Pokémon's HP and cures all status — the best healing item. Sold only at the late-game Marts, such as the Indigo Plateau lobby.

name: MAX POTION
info: Fully restores a Pokémon's HP. Sold at the later Poké Marts.

name: HYPER POTION
info: Restores 200 HP to one Pokémon. Sold at the later Poké Marts.

name: SUPER POTION
info: Restores 50 HP to one Pokémon. Sold at most Poké Marts.

name: POTION
info: A basic medicine that restores 20 HP. Stocked by nearly every Poké Mart and found throughout the early game — the cheapest healing item you can buy.

name: BOULDER BADGE
info: Item ID 0x15 — the Boulder Badge stored as a bag item. Badges are really Trainer Card flags, not items, so a Badge in the bag is a glitch state.

name: CASCADE BADGE
info: Item ID 0x16 — the Cascade Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: THUNDER BADGE
info: Item ID 0x17 — the Thunder Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: RAINBOW BADGE
info: Item ID 0x18 — the Rainbow Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: SOUL BADGE
info: Item ID 0x19 — the Soul Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: MARSH BADGE
info: Item ID 0x1A — the Marsh Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: VOLCANO BADGE
info: Item ID 0x1B — the Volcano Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: EARTH BADGE
info: Item ID 0x1C — the Earth Badge as a bag item. Badges are Trainer Card flags, not items, so this is a glitch state.

name: ESCAPE ROPE
info: Instantly warps you from a cave or dungeon back to its entrance. Sold at many Poké Marts.

name: REPEL
info: Keeps weak wild Pokémon away for 100 steps. Sold at most Poké Marts.

name: OLD AMBER
info: A fossil holding Aerodactyl's DNA, taken from the Pewter Museum. The Cinnabar Island lab can revive it into Aerodactyl. A key item with no sale price.

name: FIRE STONE
info: An evolution stone that evolves Vulpix, Growlithe and Eevee (into Flareon). Sold on the 4F of the Celadon Department Store.

name: THUNDER STONE
info: An evolution stone that evolves Pikachu and Eevee (into Jolteon). Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 4F.

name: WATER STONE
info: An evolution stone that evolves Poliwhirl, Shellder, Staryu and Eevee (into Vaporeon). Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 4F.

name: HP UP
info: A vitamin that permanently raises a Pokémon's HP stat experience. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: PROTEIN
info: A vitamin that permanently raises Attack stat experience. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: IRON
info: A vitamin that permanently raises Defense stat experience. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: CARBOS
info: A vitamin that permanently raises Speed stat experience. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: CALCIUM
info: A vitamin that permanently raises Special stat experience. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: RARE CANDY
info: Instantly raises one Pokémon by a level. No shop sells it — every Rare Candy is found in the world, hidden or lying on the ground (Mt. Moon, the Rocket Hideout, Cerulean Cave, the Safari Zone and more).

name: DOME FOSSIL
info: A fossil of Kabuto, found in Mt. Moon (you pick this or the Helix Fossil). The Cinnabar lab revives it into Kabuto. A key item with no sale price.

name: HELIX FOSSIL
info: A fossil of Omanyte, found in Mt. Moon (the alternative to the Dome Fossil). The Cinnabar lab revives it into Omanyte. A key item with no sale price.

name: SECRET KEY
info: The key to Blaine's Cinnabar Island Gym. Found deep in the Pokémon Mansion. A key item with no sale price.

name: UNUSED ITEM
info: Item ID 0x2C — an unused, unnamed item slot in the game data (the disassembly calls it ITEM_2C). Not obtainable in normal play; listed only because the slot exists.

name: BIKE VOUCHER
info: A voucher worth one bicycle, redeemable at the Cerulean Bike Shop. Given by the Pokémon Fan Club Chairman in Vermilion City. A key item with no sale price.

name: X ACCURACY
info: Raises a Pokémon's accuracy for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: LEAF STONE
info: An evolution stone that evolves Gloom, Weepinbell and Exeggcute. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 4F.

name: CARD KEY
info: An ID card that unlocks the locked doors throughout Silph Co. Found on the 5F of Silph Co. A key item with no sale price.

name: NUGGET
info: A pure-gold nugget with no use other than selling — it fetches a high price. In-game it is a found item (several are hidden or lying on the ground), and one is the reward for clearing the trainers on Nugget Bridge (Route 24).

name: PP UP 2
info: Item ID 0x32 — an unused, unnamed item slot in the game data (the disassembly calls it ITEM_32). It is not obtainable in normal play and has no defined behavior; it appears only because the slot exists.

name: POKE DOLL
info: A doll that lets you flee any wild battle instantly — famously enough to slip past the Marowak ghost in Lavender Tower. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 4F.

name: FULL HEAL
info: Cures all status conditions of one Pokémon. Sold at many Poké Marts.

name: REVIVE
info: Revives a fainted Pokémon with half its HP. Sold at the later Poké Marts.

name: MAX REVIVE
info: Revives a fainted Pokémon and fully restores its HP. Not stocked by any Mart — it is only found in the field, in places like the Pokémon Mansion, the Power Plant, Cerulean Cave and Victory Road.

name: GUARD SPEC
info: Blocks stat-lowering moves against your Pokémon for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: SUPER REPEL
info: Keeps weak wild Pokémon away for 200 steps. Sold at many Poké Marts.

name: MAX REPEL
info: Keeps weak wild Pokémon away for 250 steps. Sold at the later Poké Marts.

name: DIRE HIT
info: Raises a Pokémon's critical-hit rate for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: COIN
info: Item ID 0x3B — the Game Corner 'coin' represented as a bag item. Coins are really tracked by a separate counter (the Coin Case), not as a normal item, so a Coin sitting in the bag is a glitch state.

name: FRESH WATER
info: A bottle of water that restores 50 HP. Bought from the vending machines on the roof of the Celadon Department Store for ₽200 — also handy for the thirsty Saffron City guard, who lets you pass for a drink.

name: SODA POP
info: A fizzy soda that restores 60 HP. Sold by the Celadon Dept. Store roof vending machines for ₽300; the Saffron City guard will accept one as well.

name: LEMONADE
info: A sweet drink that restores 80 HP — the best-value roof vending-machine drink at ₽350 (Celadon Dept. Store). The Saffron City guard will take one too.

name: S.S. TICKET
info: Your boarding pass for the S.S. Anne in Vermilion City. Given by Daisy in Pallet Town after you deliver Oak's Parcel. A key item with no sale price.

name: GOLD TEETH
info: The Safari Zone Warden's lost dentures, found in the Safari Zone. Return them to him in Fuchsia City for HM04 Strength. A key item with no sale price.

name: X ATTACK
info: Raises a Pokémon's Attack for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: X DEFEND
info: Raises a Pokémon's Defense for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: X SPEED
info: Raises a Pokémon's Speed for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: X SPECIAL
info: Raises a Pokémon's Special for the current battle. Sold on the Celadon Dept. Store 5F.

name: COIN CASE
info: Holds your Game Corner coins. Given by a man in the Celadon City restaurant. A key item with no sale price.

name: OAKS PARCEL
info: A parcel for Professor Oak, bought from the Viridian City Poké Mart clerk. Deliver it to Oak in Pallet Town to receive your Pokédex. A key item with no sale price.

name: ITEM FINDER
info: A device that beeps when a hidden item is nearby. Given by one of Professor Oak's Aides on Route 11. A key item with no sale price.

name: SILPH SCOPE
info: A scope that reveals the ghostly Pokémon in Pokémon Tower. Found in the Team Rocket Hideout beneath the Celadon Game Corner. A key item with no sale price.

name: POKE FLUTE
info: An instrument that wakes any sleeping Pokémon — used to rouse the Snorlax blocking Routes 12 and 16. Given by Mr. Fuji in Lavender Town after you rescue him. A key item with no sale price.

name: LIFT KEY
info: Operates the elevator in the Team Rocket Hideout under the Celadon Game Corner, found during that dungeon. A key item with no sale price.

name: EXP ALL
info: Shares battle EXP with every party Pokémon, even ones that did not fight. Given by an Oak's Aide on Route 15. It has no sale price.

name: OLD ROD
info: A basic fishing rod that only reels in Magikarp. Given by the Fishing Guru in Vermilion City. A key item with no sale price.

name: GOOD ROD
info: A better fishing rod for catching more water Pokémon. Given by a fisherman on Route 12. A key item with no sale price.

name: SUPER ROD
info: The best fishing rod, catching the widest variety of water Pokémon. Given by a Fishing Guru on Route 12. A key item with no sale price.

name: PP UP
info: Permanently raises the maximum PP of one of a Pokémon's moves. Not sold — found in the field (e.g. Celadon, the Power Plant), so it has no shop price.

name: ETHER
info: Restores 10 PP to one of a Pokémon's moves. Not sold — found out in the field, so it has no shop price.

name: MAX ETHER
info: Fully restores PP to one of a Pokémon's moves. Not sold — found out in the field.

name: ELIXER
info: Restores 10 PP to every one of a Pokémon's moves. Not sold — found out in the field.

name: MAX ELIXER
info: Fully restores PP to every one of a Pokémon's moves. Not sold — found out in the field.

name: FLOOR B2F
info: Item ID 0x54 — an internal 'B2F' floor marker (the disassembly's FLOOR_B2F) that elevator menus use to list destinations. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR B1F
info: Item ID 0x55 — an internal 'B1F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 1F
info: Item ID 0x56 — an internal '1F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 2F
info: Item ID 0x57 — an internal '2F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 3F
info: Item ID 0x58 — an internal '3F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 4F
info: Item ID 0x59 — an internal '4F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 5F
info: Item ID 0x5A — an internal '5F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 6F
info: Item ID 0x5B — an internal '6F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 7F
info: Item ID 0x5C — an internal '7F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 8F
info: Item ID 0x5D — an internal '8F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 9F
info: Item ID 0x5E — an internal '9F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 10F
info: Item ID 0x5F — an internal '10F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR 11F
info: Item ID 0x60 — an internal '11F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: FLOOR B4F
info: Item ID 0x61 — an internal 'B4F' floor marker used by elevator menus. Not a real item, so it is flagged glitch.

name: HM 01: CUT
info: HM01 — teaches Cut, which chops down small trees in the field and is a weak Normal-type attack. Given by the Captain aboard the S.S. Anne once you cure his seasickness. HMs cannot be sold or tossed.

name: HM 02: FLY
info: HM02 — teaches Fly, which whisks you to any town you have visited and is a strong Flying-type attack. Given by a girl in a house on Route 16. HMs cannot be sold.

name: HM 03: SURF
info: HM03 — teaches Surf, letting you ride across water and a strong Water-type attack. Found in the Safari Zone's Secret House at the very back. HMs cannot be sold.

name: HM 04: STRENGTH
info: HM04 — teaches Strength, which lets you push heavy boulders and is a Normal-type attack. Given by the Safari Zone Warden in Fuchsia City once you return his Gold Teeth. HMs cannot be sold.

name: HM 05: FLASH
info: HM05 — teaches Flash, which lights up pitch-black caves such as Rock Tunnel. Given by an Oak's Aide on Route 2 once you have owned 10 or more Pokémon. HMs cannot be sold.

name: TM 01: MEGA PUNCH
info: TM01 — teaches Mega Punch, a strong Normal-type attack. Sold at the Celadon Department Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 02: RAZOR WIND
info: TM02 — teaches Razor Wind, a two-turn Normal-type attack. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 03: SWORDS DANCE
info: TM03 — teaches Swords Dance, which sharply raises the user's Attack. Found out in the world (on the ground or from an NPC).

name: TM 04: WHIRLWIND
info: TM04 — teaches Whirlwind, which fails to end wild battles in Gen 1. Found out in the world.

name: TM 05: MEGA KICK
info: TM05 — teaches Mega Kick, a strong Normal-type attack. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 06: TOXIC
info: TM06 — teaches Toxic, badly poisoning the target so the damage worsens each turn. Given by Koga, the Fuchsia City Gym Leader, when you defeat him.

name: TM 07: HORN DRILL
info: TM07 — teaches Horn Drill, a one-hit KO move. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 08: BODY SLAM
info: TM08 — teaches Body Slam, a Normal-type attack that may paralyze. Found out in the world.

name: TM 09: TAKE DOWN
info: TM09 — teaches Take Down, a strong Normal-type attack with recoil. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 10: DOUBLE EDGE
info: TM10 — teaches Double-Edge, a powerful Normal-type attack with heavy recoil. Found out in the world.

name: TM 11: BUBBLEBEAM
info: TM11 — teaches BubbleBeam, a Water-type attack that may lower Speed. Given by Misty, the Cerulean City Gym Leader, when you defeat her.

name: TM 12: WATER GUN
info: TM12 — teaches Water Gun, a basic Water-type attack. Found out in the world.

name: TM 13: ICE BEAM
info: TM13 — teaches Ice Beam, a strong Ice-type attack that may freeze. Found out in the world.

name: TM 14: BLIZZARD
info: TM14 — teaches Blizzard, a powerful Ice-type attack that may freeze. Found out in the world.

name: TM 15: HYPER BEAM
info: TM15 — teaches Hyper Beam, a devastating Normal-type attack that must recharge for a turn. Found out in the world.

name: TM 16: PAY DAY
info: TM16 — teaches Pay Day, which scatters coins for extra money after the battle. Found out in the world.

name: TM 17: SUBMISSION
info: TM17 — teaches Submission, a Fighting-type attack with recoil. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 18: COUNTER
info: TM18 — teaches Counter, which strikes back for double the physical damage taken. Found out in the world.

name: TM 19: SEISMIC TOSS
info: TM19 — teaches Seismic Toss, dealing damage equal to the user's level. Found out in the world.

name: TM 20: RAGE
info: TM20 — teaches Rage, whose power grows as the user is hit. Found out in the world.

name: TM 21: MEGA DRAIN
info: TM21 — teaches Mega Drain, a Grass-type attack that heals the user. Given by Erika, the Celadon City Gym Leader, when you defeat her.

name: TM 22: SOLARBEAM
info: TM22 — teaches SolarBeam, a powerful two-turn Grass-type attack. Found out in the world.

name: TM 23: DRAGON RAGE
info: TM23 — teaches Dragon Rage, which always deals 40 damage. Found out in the world.

name: TM 24: THUNDERBOLT
info: TM24 — teaches Thunderbolt, a strong Electric-type attack that may paralyze. Given by Lt. Surge, the Vermilion City Gym Leader, when you defeat him.

name: TM 25: THUNDER
info: TM25 — teaches Thunder, a powerful Electric-type attack that may paralyze. Found out in the world.

name: TM 26: EARTHQUAKE
info: TM26 — teaches Earthquake, a powerful Ground-type attack. Found out in the world.

name: TM 27: FISSURE
info: TM27 — teaches Fissure, a one-hit KO Ground-type move. Given by Giovanni, the Viridian City Gym Leader, when you defeat him.

name: TM 28: DIG
info: TM28 — teaches Dig, which strikes on the second turn and escapes caves outside battle. Given by the man in the Cerulean City house after a Team Rocket grunt flees through it.

name: TM 29: PSYCHIC
info: TM29 — teaches Psychic, a strong Psychic-type attack that may lower Special. Found out in the world.

name: TM 30: TELEPORT
info: TM30 — teaches Teleport, which flees wild battles and warps to the last Pokémon Center. Found out in the world.

name: TM 31: MIMIC
info: TM31 — teaches Mimic, which copies one of the target's moves. Found out in the world.

name: TM 32: DOUBLE TEAM
info: TM32 — teaches Double Team, which raises the user's evasion. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 33: REFLECT
info: TM33 — teaches Reflect, which halves incoming physical damage. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 34: BIDE
info: TM34 — teaches Bide, which waits then returns double the damage taken. Given by Brock, the Pewter City Gym Leader, when you defeat him.

name: TM 35: METRONOME
info: TM35 — teaches Metronome, which fires off a random move. Found out in the world.

name: TM 36: SELFDESTRUCT
info: TM36 — teaches Self-Destruct, a huge attack that faints the user. Found out in the world.

name: TM 37: EGG BOMB
info: TM37 — teaches Egg Bomb, a Normal-type attack. Sold at the Celadon Dept. Store TM counter (2F).

name: TM 38: FIREBLAST
info: TM38 — teaches Fire Blast, a powerful Fire-type attack that may burn. Given by Blaine, the Cinnabar Island Gym Leader, when you defeat him.

name: TM 39: SWIFT
info: TM39 — teaches Swift, a Normal-type attack that never misses. Found out in the world.

name: TM 40: SKULLBASH
info: TM40 — teaches Skull Bash, which raises Defense then attacks next turn. Found out in the world.

name: TM 41: SOFTBOILED
info: TM41 — teaches Softboiled, which heals the user and can share HP in the field. Found out in the world.

name: TM 42: DREAM EATER
info: TM42 — teaches Dream Eater, which damages a sleeping target and heals the user. Found out in the world.

name: TM 43: SKY ATTACK
info: TM43 — teaches Sky Attack, a powerful two-turn Flying-type attack. Found out in the world.

name: TM 44: REST
info: TM44 — teaches Rest, which fully heals the user but puts it to sleep for two turns. Found out in the world.

name: TM 45: THUNDER WAVE
info: TM45 — teaches Thunder Wave, which paralyzes the target. Found out in the world.

name: TM 46: PSYWAVE
info: TM46 — teaches Psywave, dealing random damage based on the user's level. Given by Sabrina, the Saffron City Gym Leader, when you defeat her.

name: TM 47: EXPLOSION
info: TM47 — teaches Explosion, an enormous attack that faints the user. Found out in the world.

name: TM 48: ROCK SLIDE
info: TM48 — teaches Rock Slide, a strong Rock-type attack. Found out in the world.

name: TM 49: TRI ATTACK
info: TM49 — teaches Tri Attack, a Normal-type attack. Found out in the world.

name: TM 50: SUBSTITUTE
info: TM50 — teaches Substitute, which sacrifices some HP to put up a decoy. Found out in the world.
